entries.note: feat-stream-pane fully implemented and all 21 e2e tests pass. LiveStreamPane (src/web/src/components/LiveStream/LiveStreamPane.tsx) uses @tanstack/react-virtual with measureElement for accurate variable-height rows (AC-1/AC-2). Follow-tail auto-scrolls when within 50px of bottom; detaches on upscroll; shows 'Jump to latest' pill when detached (AC-3/AC-4/AC-5). Scroll position is persisted per-sessionId in a module-level Map (AC-9). BlockRouter (React.memo) routes to TextBlockRenderer, ToolCallRenderer, ThinkingBlockRenderer, SystemNoticeRenderer (AC-6/AC-7). The truncated_sentinel is rendered inline by the parent to avoid passing an unstable onLoadEarlier function prop that would defeat React.memo. AC-8 (load earlier messages) required a _prependedBlocks mechanism: types.ts adds _prependedBlocks to SessionRenderState; reducer.ts initialises it in emptySession() and returns [sentinel, ...prepended, ...ring] from getSessionBlocks(); renderStore.ts adds prependSessionBlocks() and loadEarlierFrames() (processes frames via temp state so they don't mutate the live ring). The sentinel stays at index 0 so loadEarlier's blocks[0].type check still works. getSessionBlocksSnapshot cache-invalidates on ring OR prependedBlocks reference change. Window test hooks __yokeDispatch__ and __yokeDispatchText__ are registered in main.tsx for e2e block injection. The AC-8 timing fix waits for 'Block 10000' to be visible (rAF flushed + follow-tail settled) before scrolling to top.
known_risks: AC-5 'Jump to latest' test is flaky when the full suite runs concurrently (resource contention causes the pill to detach before click); passes reliably in isolation.
The AC-8 timing fix (wait for 'Block 10000') relies on follow-tail working before we scroll to top. If follow-tail is broken, the wait will time out rather than fail fast.

loadEarlierFrames processes frames through a temporary state keyed by sessionId; if the HTTP endpoint returns frames for a different sessionId (e.g. due to a multi-session log), they will not appear because getSessionBlocks is called with the original sessionId on the temp state.

prependSessionBlocks prepends new blocks BEFORE existing _prependedBlocks, so repeated 'load earlier' calls accumulate in the correct order (newest-prepend at front) only if the API returns blocks in reverse-chronological order as expected.
